# Additional Resources {#sec-references .unnumbered}

This section provides additional resources and information for working with Parkinson's disease datasets.

## Dataset-Specific Resources

### AMP PD

- [AMP-PD Official Website](https://amp-pd.org/)
- [AMP-PD in Terra](https://terra.amp-pd.org/)
- [AMP-PD FAQs](https://amp-pd.org/faqs)
- [AMP-PD News and Updates](https://amp-pd.org/news)
- **Contact**: [ACT@amp-pd.org](mailto:ACT@amp-pd.org)

### PPMI

- [PPMI Official Website](https://www.ppmi-info.org/)
- [PPMI Data Dashboard](https://www.ppmi-info.org/data-dashboard)
- [PPMI Data User Guide](https://www.ppmi-info.org/data-user-guide)
- [PPMI Study FAQs](https://www.ppmi-info.org/faqs)
- [Ongoing Specimen Analysis](https://www.ppmi-info.org/ongoing-specimen-analysis)
- [PPMI Specimen Dashboard](https://www.ppmi-info.org/specimen-dashboard)
- [LONI Data Access Platform](https://ida.loni.usc.edu/)
- **Contact**: [ppmi@michaeljfox.org](mailto:ppmi@michaeljfox.org)
- [Contact Form](https://www.ppmi-info.org/contact/contact_us)

### GP2

- [GP2 Official Website](https://gp2.org/)
- [GP2 Blog](https://gp2.org/blog/)
- [GP2 Cohort Dashboard](https://gp2.org/cohort-dashboard/)
- [GP2 Data Repository](https://gp2.org/data-repository/)
- [GP2 Training Resources](https://gp2.org/training/)
- [GP2 Code Repositories (GitHub)](https://github.com/GP2code)
- [GP2 Monogenic Resource Map](https://gp2.org/monogenic-map/)
- [GP2 Monogenic Portal](https://gp2.org/monogenic-portal/)
- [GP2 Opportunities Page](https://gp2.org/opportunities/)
- [GP2 Updates](https://gp2.org/updates/)
- **Contact**: [cohort@gp2.org](mailto:cohort@gp2.org)

### FOX INSIGHT

- [Fox Insight Official Website](https://foxinsight.michaeljfox.org/)
- [Fox DEN Data Platform](https://foxden.michaeljfox.org/)
- [Fox DEN Getting Started Video](https://foxden.michaeljfox.org/getting-started)
- [Fox DEN Variable Explorer](https://foxden.michaeljfox.org/variable-explorer)
- [Fox Insight Resources](https://foxden.michaeljfox.org/resources)
- [Fox Insight FAQs](https://foxinsight.michaeljfox.org/faqs)
- [Fox Insight Data Descriptor Publication](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-020-0401-2)
- **Contact**: [foxden@michaeljfox.org](mailto:foxden@michaeljfox.org)

## General Parkinson's Disease Resources

### Michael J. Fox Foundation

- [MJFF Official Website](https://www.michaeljfox.org/)
- [MJFF Research Tools](https://www.michaeljfox.org/research-tools)
- **Research Community**: [researchcommunity@michaeljfox.org](mailto:researchcommunity@michaeljfox.org)

### Cloud Computing Platforms

- [Terra Platform](https://terra.bio/) - Cloud platform for biomedical research
- [Verily Viewpoint Workbench](https://verily.com/solutions/workbench/) - GDPR-compliant analysis platform
- [Google Cloud Platform](https://cloud.google.com/) - Backend infrastructure for AMP-PD and GP2

## Key Publications

### FOX INSIGHT

- Smolensky, L., Amondikar, N., Crawford, K. et al. Fox Insight collects online, longitudinal patient-reported outcomes and genetic data on Parkinson's disease. *Sci Data* 7, 67 (2020). [https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-020-0401-2](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-020-0401-2)

## Community and Support

### Data Community of Practice

This guide is maintained by the Data Community of Practice, a collaborative effort to improve data accessibility and usability in Parkinson's disease research.

### Contributing to This Guide

We welcome contributions to keep this guide current and comprehensive!

::: {.callout-note}
## How to Contribute

If you would like to:

- **Add information** about a new dataset
- **Update** existing content
- **Correct** errors or outdated information
- **Suggest improvements** to the guide

Please contact: [researchcommunity@michaeljfox.org](mailto:researchcommunity@michaeljfox.org)
:::

## Technical Resources

### Data Analysis Tools

- **R Statistical Software**: [https://www.r-project.org/](https://www.r-project.org/)
- **Python**: [https://www.python.org/](https://www.python.org/)
- **Bioconductor**: [https://www.bioconductor.org/](https://www.bioconductor.org/)
- **Jupyter Notebooks**: [https://jupyter.org/](https://jupyter.org/)

### Version Control and Collaboration

- **GitHub**: [https://github.com/](https://github.com/)
- **Git**: [https://git-scm.com/](https://git-scm.com/)

### Quarto Publishing

This web book was created using Quarto, an open-source scientific and technical publishing system.

- **Quarto**: [https://quarto.org/](https://quarto.org/)
- **Quarto Books**: [https://quarto.org/docs/books/](https://quarto.org/docs/books/)

## Citing This Guide

If you use this guide in your research or reference it in publications, please cite as:

> Alosco, M., Hutchins, E., Saffie Awad, P., Dardov, V., Gottesman, J., Iwaki, H., Reyes-Perez, P., Teixeira dos Santos, D., & Sanghvi, M. (2024). *Getting Started With Parkinson's Disease Data* (Version 1.0). Data Community of Practice, The Michael J. Fox Foundation for Parkinson's Research.

## License

This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0).

You are free to:

- **Share** — copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format
- **Adapt** — remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially

Under the following terms:

- **Attribution** — You must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made

## Acknowledgements

This guide was created with contributions from the Data Community of Practice and is powered by The Michael J. Fox Foundation for Parkinson's Research.

We thank all the researchers, clinicians, and participants who contribute to these valuable datasets and make Parkinson's disease research possible.

---

**Version 1.0** | May 2024
